sevenDayForecast.headline: Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle (34U) to impact Far North Queensland on Friday.
points: Tropical Cyclone Advices and Forecast Track Maps are being issued and should be referred to for more detailed information
Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle (34U) lies in the northern Coral Sea. There is high confidence that it will continue to move west over the coming days.

Narelle (34U) is forecast to track towards and cross the far northern Queensland coast during Friday, before continuing into the Gulf of Carpentaria where there is also a High chance it will be a tropical cyclone.
Early next week Narelle (34U) may move offshore of the Kimberley coast where it has a High chance of re-developing into a tropical cyclone.

trackForecast.headline: Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle (34U) is moving steadily towards the Queensland coast, but it is expected to stay well north of Abbot Point.
trackForecast.discussion:
Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle (34U) is currently a category 4 system. It has been located using satellite imagery, and microwave imagery with good confidence. The environment is favourable for further development and Narelle is forecast to reach high-end category 4 intensity before landfall over the Cape York Peninsula, Queensland, most likely during Friday morning.

There is high confidence in the track of Narelle and it is expected to move steadily west. The system is expected to stay well north of Abbot Point with no threat of any direct wind impacts at the location.
trackForecast.discussionUpperBound: The upper bound reflects the system developing faster than anticipated and remaining over water longer.
